sigWerErrors.reason: 'ừ' and 'ờ' are both Vietnamese discourse markers with similar pragmatic meaning; the substitution does not change the overall meaning.
'nhá' and 'nhé' are both sentence-ending particles in Vietnamese with similar softening/affirmative functions; the meaning is preserved.
'mình' (I/me) is replaced by 'thì' (then/as for), which changes the subject and structure of the sentence, altering the meaning.
'lên' (up/call up) and 'liền' (immediately) have different meanings; this changes the nuance of the action but the intent to call is still present.
